result:
main decisions, in order of impact:

storage & lifecycle
- one balance per code, or ledger of transactions? ledger wins long-term (refunds, partial reversals, audit) but costs more upfront
- expiration policy (never / fixed date / inactivity) — legal varies by jurisdiction
- transferable, or bound to user account on first use?

code format & generation
- length, charset, checksum (Luhn-style) to reject typos before DB lookup
- one-time vs reusable (multi-redeem until depleted)
- printed/physical vs digital-only (affects whether you need PIN + code)

concurrency & double-spend
- two checkouts redeem same code simultaneously — need row lock or atomic decrement
- idempotency key on redeem so retries don't double-debit
- reserve-then-commit (hold balance during checkout) vs charge-on-capture only

money math
- which currency, and what if cart currency differs (FX at redeem time?)
- tax: does gift card cover tax+shipping or just subtotal? jurisdiction-dependent
- rounding rules when split with card payment
- order of operations: gift card before or after promo codes / loyalty points?

partial payment flow
- if card auth fails after gift card debited, do you reverse or hold?
- pre-auth full amount on card then settle remainder, vs split-tender at capture
- refunds: back to gift card, back to card, or proportional?

abuse & security
- rate-limit redemption attempts per IP/user (codes are guessable if short)
- don't leak "valid code, zero balance" vs "invalid code" in same response
- fraud: stolen cards used to buy gift cards then resold — flag high-value purchases

ops
- how is balance issued? purchase, promo, refund, manual admin grant — each is a separate code path
- CS tooling: lookup by code, adjust balance, reissue, void
- reporting: outstanding liability on balance sheet (real accounting concern)

biggest trap people hit: treating gift card as a discount instead of a tender. it's money, not a coupon — model it as a payment method from day one.
